description.content: Submit a document to NERD to kick off an annotation job that will be processed asynchronously. Endpoint users submit a text document and at least one knowledge base to be used to annotate the detected entities. There are two available knowledge bases, the Wikimedia knowledge base and the Capital IQ knowledge base, denoted `"wikimedia"` and `"capiq"`, respectively.

response.name: Indicates the job request was successfully received. `job_id` is returned in the response.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

response.name: Indicates a bad request because at least one knowledge base and document text must be specified.

response.name: Indicates that an unexpected error occurred and should be further investigated.

item.name: Start A NERD Annotation Job Given A Provided Job ID

request.name: Start A NERD Annotation Job Given A Provided Job ID

description.content: Notify NERD to start the annotation job given a provided job ID from uploading a text file using an upload URL. To upload a text file, please see [upload-url](/nerd/api#tag/upload-url/operation/api_annotations_upload_url) for more information.

query.description: Job identifier used to start the annotations job. Corresponds to the `job_id` returned from a GET request to the [upload-url](/nerd/api#tag/upload-url/operation/api_annotations_upload_url) endpoint.

response.name: Indicates the job request was successfully received. `job_id` is returned in the response.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

response.name: Indicates a bad request because at least one knowledge base must be specified and the document text for the provided `job_id` was not uploaded.

item.name: Get Annotations For The Specified Job ID

request.name: Get Annotations For The Specified Job ID

description.content: This endpoint returns the results of a NERD annotations job. The endpoint expects the `job_id` to be specified as the query parameter. The response contains a status, message, and the annotation results.

query.description: Job identifier used to obtain the annotations. Corresponds to the `job_id` returned from a POST request to the async annotations endpoint.

response.name: Indicates the request was successfully received and processed. Annotated data is returned in the response.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

response.name: Indicates a bad request due to 1) missing knowledge base; 2) missing document text; or 3) missing `job_id`.

response.name: Indicates that an unexpected error occurred and should be further investigated.

item.name: Delete Annotations For The Specified Job ID

request.name: Delete Annotations For The Specified Job ID

description.content: This endpoint deletes the results of a NERD annotations job. The endpoint expects the `job_id` to be specified as the query parameter. The response contains a status and message

query.description: Job identifier used to obtain the annotations. Corresponds to the `job_id` returned from a POST request to the async annotations endpoint.

response.name: Indicates the request was successfully received and processed. Annotated data is returned in the response.

item.name: Get An Upload URL For Uploading A Text File

request.name: Get An Upload URL For Uploading A Text File

description.content: This endpoint returns an upload URL, the form fields, and the job ID. The upload URL expires after **15** minutes.

response.name: Indicates that the upload URL was created successfully.
